description: Build the unified App Router shell with real navigation, login/register pages, and create Next.js API route proxies for all backend endpoints (emails, threads, contacts, calendar, repos, CI, security, admin). This is the structural foundation that lets migrated screens work.
steps: Create apps/quantmail/src/app/login/page.tsx: a real App Router login page using @quant/shared-ui (Card, Button, Input, FormField). On submit, call useAuth().login(). Show loading/error states. Include 'Forgot password' and 'Register' links. Brand-styled with dark mode support. The AuthGuard should skip guarding this route.
Create apps/quantmail/src/app/register/page.tsx: similar to login but calls apiClient.register(). After success, redirect to /login with success message.

Update apps/quantmail/src/app/page.tsx to be a proper inbox shell: Keep the existing AppShell + Sidebar pattern but add ALL navigation items (inbox, compose, thread, calendar, contacts, drive, repos, pipelines, security, settings). Each sidebar item gets an onClick that uses router.push(). Add a TopBar with app title, search, and user avatar.

Create route directories for each screen that will exist: apps/quantmail/src/app/thread/[id]/page.tsx (placeholder for now), apps/quantmail/src/app/calendar/page.tsx, apps/quantmail/src/app/contacts/page.tsx, apps/quantmail/src/app/drive/page.tsx, apps/quantmail/src/app/repos/page.tsx, apps/quantmail/src/app/repos/[id]/page.tsx, apps/quantmail/src/app/pipelines/page.tsx, apps/quantmail/src/app/security/page.tsx, apps/quantmail/src/app/settings/page.tsx. Each gets a minimal but real page using AppShell with the shared sidebar navigation, proper loading states, and data hooks.

Create API route proxies following the pattern in src/app/api/drive/files/route.ts. Create: src/app/api/emails/route.ts (GET list, POST compose), src/app/api/emails/[id]/route.ts (GET, DELETE), src/app/api/emails/[id]/send/route.ts (POST), src/app/api/emails/[id]/reply/route.ts (POST), src/app/api/emails/[id]/star/route.ts (POST), src/app/api/emails/[id]/archive/route.ts (POST), src/app/api/emails/search/route.ts (GET), src/app/api/threads/[id]/route.ts (GET), src/app/api/contacts/route.ts (GET, POST), src/app/api/contacts/[id]/route.ts (GET, PUT, DELETE), src/app/api/calendar/events/route.ts (GET, POST), src/app/api/calendar/events/[id]/route.ts (GET, PUT, DELETE), src/app/api/repos/route.ts (GET, POST), src/app/api/repos/[id]/route.ts (GET, DELETE), src/app/api/repos/[id]/pulls/route.ts (GET, POST), src/app/api/repos/[id]/issues/route.ts (GET, POST), src/app/api/ci/workflows/route.ts (GET), src/app/api/ci/builds/route.ts (GET), src/app/api/labels/route.ts (GET, POST). Each proxy follows the exact pattern of api/drive/files/route.ts: read Authorization header, forward to QUANTMAIL_BACKEND_URL (default http://localhost:3010), return NextResponse.json.
Fix the QUANTMAIL_BACKEND_URL default in all API routes to be http://localhost:3010 (not 3001).
Create a shared utility apps/quantmail/src/app/api/_lib/proxy.ts that encapsulates the proxy pattern (takes path, method, request, returns NextResponse) to DRY up all route handlers.
acceptance_criteria: /login page renders with branded UI, calls real auth on submit
/register page renders with branded UI, calls real register endpoint
All sidebar navigation items route to real pages (no dead links)
API proxy routes exist for emails, threads, contacts, calendar, repos, CI, labels
All API routes use port 3010 by default
Proxy utility helper exists and is used by all route handlers
findings: RegisterRequest type requires displayName and acceptTerms fields (not just email/password/username). Used displayName: username and acceptTerms: true as defaults. All drive API routes had localhost:3001 -- fixed to 3010 in this commit. Next.js 15 App Router dynamic route params are async (params: Promise<{id: string}>) and require await. The Sidebar component from shared-ui uses framer-motion with useReducedMotion.
